title: Config routing rules
outcome: Config routing contract established. ROUTING.md defines the three layers (shared intent in Workstreams/.agents + ontarch descriptors/policies; profile data in .chezmoidata; app-specific syntax in app configs) and the four rules (no secrets in dotfiles; no app config is a policy source of truth; cross-app rules route through .agents/ontarch; app configs consume shared profile data). .chezmoidata/routing.toml is the machine-readable contract for zed/cursor/claude/factory/opencode, each holds_secrets=false and consumes_profile_data=true. Actual per-app config templates are owned by E05; the pattern is documented.
validation: validate.sh PASS (all 7 checks) — routing covers all 5 apps, no app holds secrets, all consume shared profile data, ROUTING.md present; no-secrets scan clean; chezmoi-render DEFERRED (binary not installed)
decisions: S4 delivers the routing rules + machine-readable contract; the actual per-app config templates remain E05's scope (epic cross-epic note), so no live app config (e.g. Zed settings) is created/overwritten
routing contract is enforced by the validator (holds_secrets=false, consumes_profile_data=true for every app), not just asserted in prose
epic_status: E01 complete: S1–S4 done; chezmoi source spec at packages/panoply/dotfiles/ is dry-run clean via validate.sh. Live `chezmoi diff` across the 4 profiles is DEFERRED until chezmoi is installed (E03).
token_note: loaded 1 story block + STATE + ledger tail; no canon ref needed; compact PASS/FAIL gate output
